paper_title: Lake Werri Berri Sediment and Charcoal Analysis

methods.description: Process for collecting monolith cores from Lake Werri Berri to analyze sediment characteristics and avoid contamination.

steps.description: In June 2019, a series of monolith cores (n = 3) were taken using a spade from exposed surface peats at Lake Werri Berri.
steps.searchable_summary: collect monolith cores from Lake Werri Berri

steps.description: The monoliths were sampled to avoid any cracks to limit the possibility of contamination.

methods.description: Analysis of the longest monolith section (WB3) using the Troels-Smith method to determine bulk density and organic content.

steps.description: The longest monolith section (WB3), which was 22 cm long, was chosen for analysis and was described using a modified version of the Troels-Smith method.
steps.searchable_summary: describe monolith section using Troels-Smith method

steps.description: Bulk density was estimated by weighing a known volume of samples prior to and after oven drying (24 h at 105°C in a fanforced oven).
steps.searchable_summary: estimate bulk density by weighing samples before and after drying

steps.description: The samples were then placed in a muffle furnace for an additional 4 h at 550°C to determine loss on ignition (LOI) and the organic content was expressed as the percentage loss in mass per gramme of oven-dried sediment.
steps.searchable_summary: determine LOI and organic content using muffle furnace

methods.description: Construction of a Bayesian chronology for WB3 using radiocarbon and lead-210 dates.

steps.description: A Bayesian chronology for WB3 was constructed from radiocarbon and lead-210 dates using the R package rplum.

methods.description: Analysis of charcoal to characterize fire occurrence and intensity using various methods including FTIR spectroscopy.

steps.description: Charcoal analysis using 'macrocharcoal' (>250 µm) was performed contiguously at 1 cm intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: perform macrocharcoal analysis at 1 cm intervals

steps.description: Charcoal was separated from sediment using immersion in reverse osmosis H2O for 24 h and immersion in 4% NaOCl for 6 h.
Charcoal samples were photographed, and the charcoal particles analysed for area and count using ImageJ.
A novel method using FTIR was conducted on separated charcoal fragments to examine fire intensity.

methods.description: Construction of an 850-year composite record of select sites in eastern and south-eastern Australia using the R package Paleofire.

steps.description: A 850-year composite record was produced using the R package Paleofire following methods described in Power et al. (2008).

methods.description: Reconstruction of temperature and sea surface temperature (SST) trends using historical data and smoothing techniques.

steps.description: A temperature reconstruction of warm season land and ocean records was used to examine changes in southeast Australian temperature.
A reconstruction of the trends in recent SST for southern Australia was derived from the NOAA ERSST v5 gridded dataset.
steps.searchable_summary: reconstruct SST trends using NOAA ERSST v5
